description: Content List Facets -- Multi-dimensional facet filter panel for content list pages with 6 filter dimensions, mobile-first responsive design, and URL-persisted state

userStories.title: Install @radix-ui/react-slider and @radix-ui/react-collapsible
userStories.description: As a setup agent, I need to install the required UI primitive packages before any frontend component work begins.
acceptanceCriteria: @radix-ui/react-slider is added to client/package.json dependencies
@radix-ui/react-collapsible is added to client/package.json dependencies
npm install completes without errors from the project root
Both packages resolve correctly (no peer dependency conflicts with React 17)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Prerequisite for year/rating range sliders and collapsible accordion sections. Both packages declare react ^16.8 || ^17.0 || ^18.0 || ^19.0 as peer deps -- React 17 is supported.

userStories.title: Add creator column for TV shows (schema + metadata mapping)
userStories.description: As a database agent, I need to add a creator column to the mediaItem table and map TMDB's created_by field so the creator facet works for TV content.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration adds a `creator` column (STRING, nullable) to the mediaItem table
`creator` added to MediaItemBase as `creator?: string` in /server/src/entity/mediaItem.ts
`creator` added to `mediaItemColumns` array in /server/src/entity/mediaItem.ts
`mapTvShow()` in /server/src/metadata/provider/tmdb.ts maps `item.created_by?.map(c => c.name).join(', ')` to `tvShow.creator`
creator is stored as a plain string (same pattern as developer, not a CSV array)
Existing TV items are not backfilled (populate on next metadata refresh)
Unit tests verify: migration applies cleanly, mapTvShow populates creator from created_by array
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: TMDB already fetches created_by in TvDetailsResponse but discards it in mapTvShow(). This is a one-line mapping addition plus migration.

userStories.title: Add director column for movies (schema + metadata mapping)
userStories.description: As a database agent, I need to add a director column to the mediaItem table and extend the TMDB movie details call to include credits so the creator facet works for movie content.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration adds a `director` column (STRING, nullable) to the mediaItem table
`director` added to MediaItemBase as `director?: string` and to `mediaItemColumns` in /server/src/entity/mediaItem.ts
TMDbMovie.details() call in /server/src/metadata/provider/tmdb.ts includes `append_to_response: 'credits'` in the request params
TMDbApi.MovieDetailsResponse type extended with `credits?: { crew: TMDbApi.Crew[] }`
`mapMovie()` maps `item.credits?.crew?.filter(c => c.job === 'Director').map(c => c.name).join(', ')` to `movie.director`
director is stored as a plain string (may contain comma-separated names for co-directed films)
Existing movie items are not backfilled (populate on next metadata refresh)
Unit tests verify: migration applies cleanly, mapMovie extracts directors from credits crew where job === 'Director'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The Crew type is already defined in tmdb.ts (~lines 457-469). The details call needs append_to_response=credits added to the existing request params.

userStories.title: Add publisher column for games (schema + metadata mapping)
userStories.description: As a database agent, I need to add a publisher column to the mediaItem table and map IGDB's involved_companies publisher entries so the publisher facet works for video game content.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration adds a `publisher` column (STRING, nullable) to the mediaItem table
`publisher` added to MediaItemBase as `publisher?: string` and to `mediaItemColumns` in /server/src/entity/mediaItem.ts
`mapGame()` in /server/src/metadata/provider/igdb.ts maps publisher names from `involved_companies` entries where `publisher === true`; multiple publishers joined with ', '
publisher is stored as a plain string (same pattern as developer, not a CSV array)
Existing game items are not backfilled (populate on next metadata refresh)
Unit tests verify: migration applies cleanly, mapGame extracts publishers from involved_companies where publisher flag is true
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The involved_companies data is already fetched by the IGDB provider for the developer field. Filter entries by publisher === true, map to names, join with ', '.

userStories.title: Create creator field normalization and splitting utilities
userStories.description: As a backend utility agent, I need shared utilities for normalizing and splitting creator CSV fields so all facet aggregation and deserialization produces clean, deduplicated tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: `normalizeCreatorField(names: string[]): string[]` exists in /server/src/utils/normalizeCreators.ts -- trims whitespace from each name, filters empty strings, deduplicates while preserving order
normalizeCreatorField is applied before join(',') in OpenLibrary mapBook(), Audible mapAudiobook(), and the Goodreads import controller
`splitCreatorField(csv: string | null): string[]` splits stored CSV on ',' and trims each token, filtering empty results
splitCreatorField replaces raw .split(',') in: mediaItem.ts deserialization, items.ts mapRawResult, and details.ts deserialization
Unit tests cover: multiple authors, single author, empty string, null, names with leading/trailing whitespace, confirmed 'First Last' format preserved
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: All name sources confirmed 'First Last' format -- no Last,First conversion needed. Normalization is defensive trimming and dedup only. splitCreatorField handles the leading-space problem from join(', ') storage pattern.

userStories.title: Add multi-facet filter params to paginated items endpoint
userStories.description: As a backend API agent, I need the paginated items endpoint to accept multi-value and range facet parameters so the frontend can fetch filtered result sets across all 6 dimensions plus media type and status.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/items/paginated accepts `genres` param as comma-separated string (e.g., genres=Action,Comedy)
GET /api/items/paginated accepts `languages` param as comma-separated string
GET /api/items/paginated accepts `creators` param as comma-separated string
GET /api/items/paginated accepts `publishers` param as comma-separated string
GET /api/items/paginated accepts `mediaTypes` param as comma-separated string filtering to matching media types
GET /api/items/paginated accepts `yearMin` and `yearMax` as integer params filtering by releaseDate year
GET /api/items/paginated accepts `ratingMin` and `ratingMax` as float params (0-10) filtering by tmdbRating

GET /api/items/paginated accepts `status` param as comma-separated keys (rated,unrated,watchlist,seen) mapping to existing boolean flags: rated -> onlyWithUserRating, unrated -> onlyWithoutUserRating, watchlist -> onlyOnWatchlist, seen -> onlySeenItems
Multi-value genres, languages, creators, publishers, mediaTypes apply OR logic within each dimension
All active dimensions combine with AND logic (e.g., genres=Action AND language=en)
yearMin/yearMax and ratingMin/ratingMax apply as inclusive bounds
Items with no tmdbRating excluded when ratingMin > 0
Knex query uses LIKE '%genre%' per selected genre (OR-chained orWhere) -- genres column is CSV, not JSON
publishers filter uses exact equality check against publisher column (plain string, not CSV)
All new params are optional; omitting them does not change existing behavior
Existing singular `genre` param continues to work independently alongside plural `genres`
Legacy `filter` param continues to work when `status` param is absent
Unit tests verify: each filter param independently, combined filters with AND/OR logic, empty/absent params return all results, backward compat with existing genre and filter params
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the existing single-value genre LIKE pattern for the plural genres param. The existing `year` URL param filters by seen-date year (not releaseDate) -- do not conflict with yearMin/yearMax. Add yearMin, yearMax, ratingMin, ratingMax, genres, languages, creators, publishers, mediaTypes, status as new optional fields on GetItemsArgs.

userStories.title: Create facet counts API endpoint
userStories.description: As a backend API architect agent, I need to design and implement the facet counts endpoint that returns available facet options with item counts across all dimensions, scoped to the current user's library. This is the most architecturally complex backend story -- leverage the software-architect agent for query design if the aggregation logic is unclear.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/items/facets endpoint exists on ItemsController and returns { genres, years, languages, creators, publishers, mediaTypes } each as Array<{ value: string; count: number }>
Endpoint accepts optional mediaType query param; when provided, scopes to that type and omits mediaTypes; when absent, aggregates all types and includes mediaTypes

Endpoint accepts same filter params as GET /api/items/paginated (genres, languages, creators, publishers, yearMin, yearMax, ratingMin, ratingMax, status, search, filter) so counts reflect currently filtered set
Each dimension sorted by count descending
Options with zero counts excluded from response
years dimension returns individual year values derived from releaseDate

creators dimension aggregates from director (movies), creator (TV), authors (books/audiobooks), developer (games) using splitCreatorField(); on mixed-content (no mediaType) unions all four into single list
publishers dimension populated from publisher column; included when mediaType=video_game or when no mediaType; hidden for other single-type pages
Genres aggregation uses application-layer splitting (fetch rows, split in TypeScript) -- NOT json_each() -- because genres column is CSV
Facets query scopes to current user's library by joining same listItem/seen subqueries used by getItemsKnexSql -- does NOT query raw mediaItem table
Run `npm run build:routes` after adding getFacets to ItemsController -- verify client typed API method is generated in rest-api/index.ts
Unit tests verify: response shape, mediaType scoping, count accuracy, zero-count exclusion, user-scoping prevents cross-user data leakage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Most complex backend story. The query must mirror getItemsKnexSql's user-scoping joins. Genre splitting must happen in TS application layer, not SQL, because genres is a CSV column. The server uses typescript-routes-to-openapi -- build:routes regenerates routes.ts and rest-api/index.ts.

userStories.title: Create useFacets hook and useMultiValueSearchParam
userStories.description: As a frontend infrastructure agent, I need to create the core state management hooks that read/write all facet URL params with a merge strategy that preserves existing params, providing setter functions and derived state for the entire facet feature.
acceptanceCriteria: `useMultiValueSearchParam` hook created in /client/src/hooks/ -- reads/writes comma-separated multi-value URL params for genres, languages, creators, publishers, status, mediaTypes
`useFacets()` hook created in /client/src/hooks/facets.ts as single source of truth for all facet URL state
useFacets reads/writes: genres, yearMin, yearMax, ratingMin, ratingMax, languages, creators, publishers, status, mediaTypes
useFacets returns: active facet query params object (for API calls), activeFacetCount number, and individual setter functions per dimension including setPublishers and setMediaTypes
All setter functions use merge strategy -- spread all existing searchParams before writing new value; never call setSearchParams with bare object
orderBy and sortOrder URL params are explicitly preserved by all facet setters (never dropped)
Any facet change resets pagination to page 1 via the existing handleArgumentChange callback pattern
useFacets does NOT own orderBy or sortOrder -- those remain managed exclusively by useOrderByComponent
Unit tests verify: param read/write round-trip, merge strategy preserves all existing params including orderBy/sortOrder, pagination resets on facet change, activeFacetCount accuracy
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Separate from existing scalar useUpdateSearchParams to avoid breaking OrderBy, pagination, and useMenuComponent. useFacets is the foundation for all frontend facet components. useFilterBy hook is no longer called when showFacets={true}.

userStories.title: Create FacetPanel desktop sidebar layout with accordion structure
userStories.description: As a frontend component agent, I need to build the FacetPanel container with collapsible accordion sections, the FacetSection wrapper, and the shared ExpandableList component -- rendering as a persistent 240px sidebar on desktop.
acceptanceCriteria: FacetPanel component created in /client/src/components/Facets/FacetPanel.tsx
FacetSection component created in /client/src/components/Facets/FacetSection.tsx using @radix-ui/react-collapsible for keyboard nav and ARIA
ExpandableList component created in /client/src/components/Facets/ExpandableList.tsx -- shared 15-item truncation with 'Show more / Show less' toggle
FacetPanel renders as 240px fixed-width sidebar beside the grid on screens >= 1024px (flex layout: sidebar + grid in a row)
Sidebar is always visible on desktop; no button required to open
Each facet dimension renders as a collapsible FacetSection with chevron toggle
Chevron rotates 180 degrees when expanded (CSS transform, no JS animation)
Sections start collapsed; auto-expand when they have active selections
'Clear all filters' button appears at top of panel only when at least one facet is active
Clicking 'Clear all' resets all facet URL params via useFacets and collapses all sections
Uses existing TailwindCSS dark theme and accent color for checked/active states
All user-facing strings wrapped with @lingui/macro t or <Trans>
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using Selenium MCP
userStories.notes: Creates the shell component structure. Individual facet section content (genre, year, etc.) will be added by subsequent stories. The grid area shrinks to accommodate the sidebar on desktop.

userStories.title: Create FacetPanel mobile bottom drawer
userStories.description: As a frontend component agent, I need to implement the mobile-responsive variant of the FacetPanel as a bottom sheet drawer that opens from a toolbar Filters button, with live grid updates on selection.
acceptanceCriteria: On screens < 1024px, FacetPanel sidebar is hidden
A 'Filters' button renders in the list toolbar area near existing OrderBy controls
Filters button visually matches existing OrderBy button style
When any facet is active, Filters button shows a badge with count of active facet dimensions
Tapping Filters opens a bottom sheet drawer with full FacetPanel content
Drawer uses position:fixed bottom:0 left:0 right:0 with high z-index
Drawer mounted via existing Portal component (/client/src/components/Portal.tsx)
Drawer has visible 'Close' button at top and closes on backdrop tap
Selecting a facet inside drawer immediately updates background grid (no 'Apply' button needed)
Drawer is scrollable if facet content exceeds viewport height
All user-facing strings wrapped with @lingui/macro t or <Trans>
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using Selenium MCP
userStories.notes: No animated transitions required (functional behavior only; enhancement deferred). FacetPanel content inside drawer is the same accordion structure as the desktop sidebar.

acceptanceCriteria: FacetCheckboxList component created in /client/src/components/Facets/FacetCheckboxList.tsx reusing existing Checkbox from /client/src/components/Checkbox.tsx

Genres sorted by count descending
'Show more' link expands to all genres; 'Show less' collapses back to 15
Multiple genres can be checked simultaneously (OR logic)
Checked items are visually distinct (filled checkbox + accent color)
Count badges update reactively when other facets change (React Query refetch on param change)
Selecting a genre updates URL param genres as comma-separated values and resets page to 1
All user-facing strings wrapped with @lingui/macro t or <Trans>

userStories.title: Implement year range facet section with dual-handle slider

Year FacetSection shows dual-handle range slider spanning from earliest available year to current year

Dragging either handle updates corresponding numeric input in real-time (onValueChange)
Typing into numeric input updates slider on blur
URL params yearMin and yearMax written on slider release (onValueCommit), not on every drag step
Input values clamped to available year range; invalid inputs revert to nearest valid value

userStories.description: As a frontend component agent, I need to implement the TMDB rating range facet section reusing the FacetRangeSlider with a 0-10 range and 0.5-step increments.

userStories.title: Implement creator facet section with context-aware labels
userStories.description: As a frontend component agent, I need to implement the creator facet section with media-type-aware labels (Director/Creator/Author/Developer) so users can browse a specific person's body of work across all content types.
acceptanceCriteria: Creator FacetSection renders with context-aware label: 'Director' for movies, 'Creator' for TV, 'Author' for books and audiobooks, 'Developer' for games, generic 'Creator' on mixed-content pages
Section hidden when current media type has no creator data in facet response
Checkbox list with count badges using FacetCheckboxList and ExpandableList (up to 15, Show more/less)
Multiple creators can be selected simultaneously (OR logic)
Selecting a creator updates URL param creators as comma-separated values and resets page to 1
All user-facing strings wrapped with @lingui/macro t or <Trans>

Tests pass

userStories.notes: Label mapping depends on the mediaType prop passed to FacetPanel. On mixed-content pages (no mediaType), backend returns a union of all creator fields and the generic 'Creator' label is used.

userStories.title: Implement status facet section (replaces FilterByComponent)

acceptanceCriteria: Status FacetSection renders checkboxes: 'Rated', 'Unrated', 'On watchlist', and a media-type-aware seen label ('Watched' for movies/TV, 'Played' for games, 'Read' for books, 'Listened' for audiobooks)
Multiple status values can be checked simultaneously (AND logic -- item must match all)
Selecting statuses updates URL param status as comma-separated keys (e.g., status=rated,watchlist)
Internal keys: rated, unrated, watchlist, seen
When showFacets={true}, FilterByComponent is NOT rendered in the toolbar -- Status facet replaces it entirely
When showFacets={false} (e.g., statistics pages), FilterByComponent continues to render unchanged -- no regression
Legacy filter URL param preserved for backward compatibility: if filter present and status absent, backend treats as before
All user-facing strings wrapped with @lingui/macro t or <Trans>

Tests pass
Verify in browser using Selenium MCP

Section NOT rendered on movies, TV, books, or audiobooks single-type pages
Section header reads 'Publisher'

Publishers sorted by count descending

userStories.title: Implement active facet chips row with individual dismissal

Year and rating ranges each produce a single chip showing the range (not individual chips per bound)

'Clear all' button at end of chip row resets all facets and URL params

userStories.title: Verify sort order compatibility with active facets
userStories.description: As a QA agent, I need to verify and ensure that the existing OrderByComponent and all 10 sort fields remain fully functional when facets are active, and that facet interactions never drop or reset sort params.
acceptanceCriteria: OrderByComponent continues to render in PaginatedGridItems toolbar when showFacets={true} -- it is NOT removed or relocated
On mobile, sort direction toggle button and sort field menu remain accessible alongside Filters button
All 10 MediaItemOrderBy sort fields (title, releaseDate, lastSeen, status, mediaType, progress, recommended, nextAiring, lastAiring, unseenEpisodes) work when facets are active
Both SortOrder directions (asc, desc) work correctly with any active facet selection
orderBy and sortOrder URL params are preserved by all useFacets setter functions -- never dropped
Changing a facet does NOT reset orderBy or sortOrder to their defaults
Any sort change still resets pagination to page 1 (existing behavior preserved, not broken)
Unit tests verify: facet setter preserves orderBy/sortOrder params, sort change with active facets returns correctly ordered results

Tests pass
Verify in browser using Selenium MCP
userStories.notes: No changes to OrderBy.tsx or useOrderByComponent are required. The merge requirement is enforced at the useFacets setter level only. useOrderByComponent already uses its own merge strategy via useUpdateSearchParams.

userStories.title: Integrate FacetPanel into PaginatedGridItems and enable on content pages
userStories.description: As an integration agent, I need to wire the completed FacetPanel, ActiveFacetChips, and all facet sections into PaginatedGridItems via the showFacets prop, enable it on ItemsPage/WatchlistPage/UpcomingPage/InProgressPage, set up parallel API queries, and implement cross-type navigation param forwarding.
acceptanceCriteria: PaginatedGridItems accepts new optional showFacets?: boolean prop
When showFacets={true}, PaginatedGridItems renders FacetPanel alongside grid (desktop sidebar layout + mobile drawer)
When showFacets={true}, ActiveFacetChips row renders between toolbar and grid
When showFacets={true}, FilterByComponent is NOT rendered in toolbar (Status facet replaces it)
/api/items/facets and /api/items/paginated called in parallel (two separate useQuery calls) on facet param change
keepPreviousData: true applied to both queries to prevent flash of empty state during refetch
staleTime: 30000 applied to facets query only; items query always re-fetches on param change
ItemsPage passes showFacets={true} to PaginatedGridItems
WatchlistPage passes showFacets={true} to PaginatedGridItems
UpcomingPage and InProgressPage pass showFacets={true} if they use PaginatedGridItems; deferred with follow-up ticket if they don't
On mixed-content pages (Watchlist, Upcoming, In Progress), useFacets passes no mediaType to facets API; Media Type section renders; creator section shows union labeled 'Creator'
Nav links between single-type pages (/movies, /tv, /games, /books, /audiobooks) carry forward: genres, yearMin, yearMax, ratingMin, ratingMax, languages, orderBy, sortOrder
Nav links between single-type pages DROP: creators, publishers, status on cross-type navigation (creators field source changes, publishers is games-specific, status labels change per type)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using Selenium MCP
userStories.notes: This is the final wiring story that brings all facet components together. All individual components (US-009 through US-019) must be complete. The grid area shrinks to accommodate the sidebar on desktop via flex layout.

userStories.title: Extract and compile i18n translations for all 28 language files

Run lingui extract to pull new message entries into catalog files

English translations are complete and accurate
Non-English translations use English string as placeholder (matching project convention for new keys)
No existing translations modified or removed
lingui compile runs without errors

Tests pass

userStories.title: Integration tests for Content List Facets feature
userStories.description: As a QA agent, I need comprehensive integration tests that verify all product requirements end-to-end, covering the complete user journey from facet selection through grid filtering across all media types, page contexts, responsive layouts, and URL persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test: User applies genre facet on movies page -- grid shows only matching movies, chip appears, count badges update
Integration test: User applies multiple facets simultaneously (genre + year range + language) -- AND logic between dimensions, OR within genre dimension
Integration test: Year range slider updates URL params on release and grid filters correctly by releaseDate year
Integration test: Rating range slider excludes items with no tmdbRating when ratingMin > 0
Integration test: Creator facet shows 'Director' label on movies page, 'Author' on books page, generic 'Creator' on watchlist (mixed-content)
Integration test: Publisher facet renders on games page and mixed-content pages, hidden on movies/TV/books/audiobooks pages
Integration test: Status facet replaces FilterBy -- Rated/Unrated/Watchlist/Seen checkboxes filter correctly with AND logic
Integration test: Media Type facet renders on mixed-content pages and filters grid to selected types
Integration test: Individual chip dismissal removes that specific facet and updates grid; 'Clear all' resets everything
Integration test: Sort order (orderBy + sortOrder) preserved through facet apply and remove cycles -- never reset
Integration test: Cross-type navigation (movies -> TV) carries forward genres/year/rating/language/sort but drops creators/publishers/status
Integration test: URL params are bookmarkable -- reloading page with facet params fully restores filtered state
Integration test: Mobile drawer opens from Filters button, facets work inside drawer, drawer closes on backdrop tap
Integration test: Facet counts API and items API fire in parallel on each facet change
All unit tests from previous stories still pass
Integration tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Runs AFTER all feature stories complete. Validates complete implementation end-to-end. Use Selenium MCP for browser-based integration tests covering responsive layouts and user interactions.
